title: ADP Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**ADP** is an enterprise HCM / Payroll / Time / Benefits / Talent suite. ADP Marketplace exposes a REST API at `https://api.adp.com` for consumer applications. This connector ‚Äî `AdpConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "adp"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_client_credentials"`) ‚Äî wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from an ADP practitioner account:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Workers (HCM) | `/hr/v2/workers` + `/hr/v2/employees` | List + read workers, employees, business communications |
| Payroll | `/payroll/v1/workers/{aoid}/pay-statements` + `/payroll/v1/workers/{aoid}/pay-distributions` | List pay statements, fetch a single statement, list direct-deposit accounts |
| Time | `/time/v2/workers/{aoid}/time-cards` + `/time-off/v2/workers/{aoid}/time-off-requests` | List time cards, list / submit time-off requests |
| Benefits | `/benefits/v1/workers/{aoid}/enrollments` | List a worker's benefit enrollments |
| Talent | `/hr/v2/jobs` + `/core/v1/organization-units` | List jobs, list org units |

The connector normalises workers, pay statements and time-off requests into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC ‚Äî all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::ADPHTTPClient` which also owns the OAuth2 token cache + the mTLS handshake).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Register the consumer application in ADP Marketplace

1. Sign in to https://adp.com ‚Üí **Marketplace ‚Üí My Apps ‚Üí Add Application**.
2. ADP issues a **Client ID** + **Client Secret** (OAuth2 client-credentials).
3. **Generate a TLS client certificate** in the ADP developer portal ‚Äî ADP issues a `.crt` (certificate chain) and `.key` (private key) pair. The certificate is bound to the application; rotation is operator-driven.
4. Note the **base URL** (`https://api.adp.com`) and **token URL** (`https://accounts.adp.com/auth/oauth/v2/token`) ‚Äî overridable for ADP partner sandboxes.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP ‚Üí Manage Connectors ‚Üí ADP**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `client_id` | OAuth2 client id from step 2 |
| `client_secret` (secret) | OAuth2 client secret from step 2 |
| `client_cert` (textarea) | Paste the **entire** PEM cert chain (`-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----` ‚Ä¶ `-----END CERTIFICATE-----`) |
| `client_key` (textarea) | Paste the **entire** PEM private key (`-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----` ‚Ä¶ `-----END PRIVATE KEY-----`) |
| `base_url` (optional) | Override `https://api.adp.com` for a partner sandbox |
| `token_url` (optional) | Override `https://accounts.adp.com/auth/oauth/v2/token` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (optional, default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates the four required fields, **writes the PEM material to a tmp file pair** (so httpx can load them via `cert=(crt_path, key_path)`), and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, PENDING)`. The next call (`authenticate()` or `health_check()`) mints the OAuth2 token over mTLS.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `GET /hr/v2/workers?$top=1`. On 2xx ‚Üí `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 ‚Üí `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 ‚Üí `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 ‚Üí `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with a structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
ADP Marketplace requires **two layers of credentials simultaneously** (`AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_client_credentials"`):

1. **OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials grant** (machine-to-machine) ‚Äî `POST {token_url}` with `grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=‚Ä¶&client_secret=‚Ä¶`.
2. **Mutual TLS (mTLS)** ‚Äî every TLS handshake (including the token mint and every resource call) presents the ADP-issued client cert + private key.

### Credential model

- `client_id` + `client_secret` are sent **only** to the token endpoint in an `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` body.
- `client_cert` + `client_key` are inline PEM strings the connector stores at install. At install they are projected to a tmp file pair (`chmod 600`) so httpx can load them through its `cert=(crt_path, key_path)` API.
- The minted access token is cached in-memory until **60 seconds before** its declared `expires_in`. Concurrent callers serialize on an `asyncio.Lock` so we never mint twice.
- ADP issues no `refresh_token` ‚Äî on 401 the cached token is invalidated and the next call re-mints automatically.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `client_id` + `client_secret` are non-empty, the inline PEMs are shape-valid (or `cert_path`/`key_path` already exist), and projects PEM to tmp. Does **not** call any ADP endpoint. |
| `authorize(auth_code, state)` | No-op for client-credentials ‚Äî delegates to `authenticate()`. |
| `authenticate()` | Mints a fresh access token via the client-credentials grant over mTLS. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /hr/v2/workers?$top=1` ‚Äî minimal probe; exercises both the token cache and the mTLS handshake. |
| on-401 inside `_request` | Invalidate cached token + retry the call once with a freshly-minted token. |

### Header contract

Token mint:
```
POST {token_url}
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Accept:       application/json

grant_type=client_credentials&client_id={CID}&client_secret={CSEC}
```

Resource call:
```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```
The TLS handshake under both presents the client cert/key ‚Äî `cert=(crt_path, key_path)` on `httpx.AsyncClient`.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` ‚Äî never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `client_id` | text (secret-classified) | yes | `self.client_id` | OAuth2 client id |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | `self.client_secret` | OAuth2 client secret |
| `client_cert` | textarea | yes | `self._client_cert_pem` ‚Üí tmp file ‚Üí `self.cert_path` | Inline PEM cert chain |
| `client_key` | textarea | yes | `self._client_key_pem` ‚Üí tmp file ‚Üí `self.key_path` | Inline PEM private key |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (default `https://api.adp.com`) | Override for sandbox |
| `token_url` | text | no | `self.token_url` (default `https://accounts.adp.com/auth/oauth/v2/token`) | Override for sandbox |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["client_id", "client_secret", "client_cert", "client_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_ADP_BASE      = "https://api.adp.com"
_ADP_TOKEN_URL = "https://accounts.adp.com/auth/oauth/v2/token"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `AdpConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::ADPHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validates the 4 required fields, projects inline PEM to tmp, initialises `ADPHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the ADP API.

### `async authorize(auth_code='', state='') -> TokenInfo`
No-op for client-credentials ‚Äî delegates to `authenticate()`.

### `async authenticate() -> TokenInfo`
Mints a fresh access token via the client-credentials grant over mTLS. Calls `set_token(...)` so BaseConnector persists it.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists 1 worker via `GET /hr/v2/workers?$top=1`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates ADP workers (`$top=100`, walking `$skip`) ‚Üí `NormalizedDocument` ‚Üí `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Workers (HCM)
children.content:
### `async list_workers(*, top=100, skip=0, filter=None, select=None) -> Dict`
`GET /hr/v2/workers?$top=‚Ä¶&$skip=‚Ä¶&$filter=‚Ä¶&$select=‚Ä¶`.

### `async get_worker(aoid: str) -> Dict`
`GET /hr/v2/workers/{aoid}`.

### `async list_employees(*, top=100, skip=0, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /hr/v2/employees?$top=‚Ä¶&$skip=‚Ä¶&$filter=‚Ä¶`.

### `async get_employee(aoid: str) -> Dict`
`GET /hr/v2/employees/{aoid}`.

### `async list_business_communications(worker_aoid: str) -> Dict`
`GET /hr/v2/workers/{aoid}/business-communications` ‚Äî list emails / phones.

### `async update_personal_communications(worker_aoid, email=None, phone=None) -> Dict`
`POST /events/hr/v1/worker.business-communication.email.change` ‚Äî Events envelope.

children.title: Payroll
children.content:
### `async list_payments(worker_aoid, *, top=50, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /payroll/v1/workers/{aoid}/pay-statements?$top=‚Ä¶&$filter=‚Ä¶`.

### `async get_payment_outputs(worker_aoid, pay_statement_id) -> Dict`
`GET /payroll/v1/workers/{aoid}/pay-statements/{id}`.

### `async list_pay_distributions(worker_aoid) -> Dict`
`GET /payroll/v1/workers/{aoid}/pay-distributions` ‚Äî direct-deposit accounts.

children.title: Time
children.content:
### `async list_time_cards(worker_aoid, *, top=50, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /time/v2/workers/{aoid}/time-cards?$top=‚Ä¶&$filter=‚Ä¶`.

### `async list_time_off_requests(worker_aoid, *, top=50) -> Dict`
`GET /time-off/v2/workers/{aoid}/time-off-requests?$top=‚Ä¶`.

### `async submit_time_off_request(worker_aoid, policy_code, start_date, end_date, hours=None, comments=None) -> Dict`
`POST /time-off/v2/workers/{aoid}/time-off-requests` ‚Äî body built by `helpers/utils.py::build_time_off_event`.

children.title: Benefits + Talent
children.content:
### `async list_benefits(worker_aoid, *, top=50) -> Dict`
`GET /benefits/v1/workers/{aoid}/enrollments`.

### `async list_jobs(*, top=100, filter=None) -> Dict`
`GET /hr/v2/jobs?$top=‚Ä¶&$filter=‚Ä¶`.

### `async list_organizational_units(*, top=100) -> Dict`
`GET /core/v1/organization-units?$top=‚Ä¶`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
ADPError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ADPAuthError                      # 401 / 403 (also: token mint failures)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ADPBadRequestError                # 400
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ADPNotFoundError                  # 404
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ADPConflictError                  # 409
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ADPRateLimitError                 # 429 ‚Äî retry_after_s
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ADPServerError                    # 5xx
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ ADPAPIError                       # any other 4xx not classified above
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
ADPNetworkError = ADPServerError      # legacy name from older code
ADPNotFound     = ADPNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 401 | Invalidate cached token + retry **once** with a freshly-minted token; if the second call also 401s, raise |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After` header (numeric seconds); else exponential backoff `0.5 * 2^(attempt-1)`. Retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Exponential backoff retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.RequestError` | Exponential backoff retry up to 3, then `ADPNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 ‚Üí ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 ‚Üí ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 ‚Üí ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   ‚Äî logged as warning
other ‚Üí ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED) or OFFLINE on network error
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | One of `client_id`, `client_secret`, `client_cert`, `client_key` is blank or fails the PEM shape check | Re-enter the four required fields. The cert must contain `-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----` and the key must contain `-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----` (or `BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY` / `BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY`). |
| `Token mint failed (401)` from `authenticate()` | OAuth2 `client_id` / `client_secret` wrong, OR the TLS handshake failed because the cert isn't tied to that client | Rotate the client secret in ADP Marketplace; re-issue the cert/key pair from the developer portal; re-paste both PEMs into ACP. |
| `Token mint network error: ‚Ä¶SSL‚Ä¶` | The PEM material is malformed OR the key doesn't match the cert OR `cert_path` / `key_path` aren't readable | Confirm both PEMs were pasted in full including BEGIN/END markers; check the tmp file is `chmod 600`; verify cert and key are a matching pair (`openssl x509 -modulus -in client.crt`, `openssl rsa -modulus -in client.key` ‚Äî moduli must match). |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from ADP ‚Äî consumer application not entitled to `/hr/v2/workers` | In ADP Marketplace check your app's API entitlements; subscribe to **Workers v2** before calling other surfaces. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 even after the one-shot refresh ‚Äî token mint itself is failing | Same as 'Token mint failed (401)' above. |
| `adp.http.retry` warnings spike on 429 | Hitting the ADP per-minute rate cap (default 60/min on Marketplace tier) | The client honours `Retry-After` then exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` in install_fields or batch fewer calls per minute. |
| 404 on `/hr/v2/workers/{aoid}` | The associate OID doesn't exist in the practitioner's company OR the OID is from another company | Confirm the OID with `list_workers(top=‚Ä¶)`; ADP enforces company-scope at the API layer. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'ADPNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed ‚Äî `ADPNetworkError = ADPServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| TLS connect failure in production but tests pass | `respx` intercepts at the transport layer so tests skip the real mTLS handshake; production goes through the real network ‚Äî confirm the project's egress allowlist permits `api.adp.com` and `accounts.adp.com` on TCP/443 (see `vault_egress_allowlist_prod_requirement.md`). | Add both hosts to `VAULT_EGRESS_ALLOWLIST`. |
